justification: All 6 required rules identified with correct invariant classification (BR-1/BR-5 license=true, BR-2 double-booking=true, BR-6 visibility=true, BR-7/BR-8 note locking covered, BR-3/BR-4 cancellation role boundary captured). Agent went beyond minimum with 8 rules total. Regulatory rules correctly marked invariant:true. 11 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum), covering all required scenarios. Role-based cancellation correctly split into patient (BR-3, invariant:false) and admin (BR-4, invariant:false) rules. Confidence at 82 with clearly stated assumptions about license scope, boundary conditions, and access revocation.

observations.text: Exceeded minimum rule count (8 vs 6 required) with thoughtful separation of BR-7 (48h window policy) from BR-8 (immutability of locked notes)
Invariant classifications are precise — BR-7 (48h threshold) correctly marked false as a tunable policy, while BR-8 (locked note immutability) correctly marked true as a legal/audit requirement
11 acceptance criteria with clear Given/When/Then structure covering all required scenarios plus edge cases like admin vs locked notes
Excellent edge cases section addressing license revocation, boundary timing, timezone handling, and cancelled-appointment access revocation
Confidence level 82 is well-calibrated with specific low-confidence areas and stated assumptions
Rich ubiquitous language section establishes precise domain vocabulary

Minor: BR-3 cancellation time window could arguably be invariant:true for the role boundary aspect (rubric says 'invariant: true for the role boundary'), though the agent's split into BR-3/BR-4 effectively captures this distinction differently
